content:
Mod's reply: https://discord.com/channels/1131791637933199470/1170692781216378960/1468592772809494610 

Requesting confirmation from the admin on whether server rules apply to places outside of this Discord server. If yes, then I further request that the server rules be updated to include this. This request was sparked by the recent ban of user .kleinod, who has been accused of violating this Discord server's rules in another Discord server. 

If the content was shared within this Discord server, it should be judged under server rules. However, if it wasn't, then it is debatable whether moderation action is needed. Thus, I want clarification from the server admin.

To clarify, our jurisdiction is limited to this server. We should not be passing judgment on members for their actions in other communities unless that drama is intentionally brought into our channels.

Regarding the ban of .kleinod: I do not currently have the full context or evidence regarding why this user was banned. We will be looking into the details of the case further before making a final decision on the ban's duration, whether to keep it, shorten it, or lift it.

In the meantime, I agree with your suggestion to update the rules. I will be adding our guidelines stating that we only enforce our rules within this Discord, except in extreme cases where external actions (like doxxing or raiding) directly threaten our members safety.

good stance, skimmed through the drama and it seems dumb to be punishing people for unrelated remarks in other communities

that said it makes sense to enforce rules if it affects this discord or is related to it (i.e. content creators posting logs from this discord to have their fanbase attack users here or directly attacking/insulting discord users in their vids is a problem imo even if it is happening outside of the discord)
